name: Styra DAS (Declarative Authorization Service) API
what_it_does: Enterprise policy-as-code management platform built on OPA (Open Policy Agent) — enabling platform and security teams to centrally author, test, distribute, and monitor OPA policies for Kubernetes admission control, API authorization, microservice authZ, and data access control, with a REST API for policy CRUD, decision log streaming, and system management, serving as the enterprise control plane for OPA deployments.
use_cases: Managing Kubernetes admission control policies via Styra DAS for cluster security governance
Centralizing OPA policy distribution via Styra DAS across multiple OPA instances and clusters
Auditing authorization decisions via Styra decision log API for compliance reporting
Enforcing API authorization policies via Styra DAS Envoy integration for microservices
Automating policy lifecycle via Styra REST API for GitOps-based policy management
not_for: Simple RBAC without policy complexity (use native Kubernetes RBAC for basic access control)
Authentication (Styra/OPA handles authorization only — use Auth0/Okta for authn)
Teams without Rego expertise or OPA adoption (high learning curve)
best_when: An enterprise platform team needs centralized OPA policy management across multiple Kubernetes clusters or microservices — where Styra DAS provides the control plane, policy distribution, and decision audit logging at scale.
avoid_when: You're just starting with OPA, have a single cluster, or need simple RBAC without policy-as-code complexity.

auth.notes: API token via Authorization: Bearer header. Tokens generated in Styra DAS console with role-based access. No OAuth2. SSO/SAML for user login (enterprise feature). Service account tokens for OPA agents connecting to DAS. Token-based access to system-specific policy bundles. Webhook notifications for policy change events.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: Styra Free — limited to 1 system, community features
pricing.notes: Founded by OPA creators (Torin Sandall, Tim Hinrichs, Patrick East) from Google/Styra. US company (Redwood City, CA). $40M+ funding. OPA (Open Policy Agent) is CNCF graduated open-source project (Apache 2.0). Styra DAS is enterprise SaaS control plane for OPA. Rego policy language for policy authoring. Kubernetes: Styra integrates as OPA Gatekeeper or standalone OPA via admission webhook. Decision logging: stream policy decisions to Styra for audit. Impact analysis: preview policy changes before deployment. Policy bundles distributed to OPA agents via bundle API. Acquired by Palo Alto Networks in 2023.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise SLAs available

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with Bearer token. OPA/Rego knowledge required. Enterprise pricing. Free tier limited. Acquired by Palo Alto Networks 2023. Policy-as-code expertise needed. Decision log streaming.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Policy reads idempotent by policy path. System reads idempotent by system ID. Policy updates: use PUT for idempotent updates. Decision logs: append-only stream.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires OPA/Rego expertise — Rego policy language has steep learning curve
DAS is a control plane — OPA agents must be deployed separately in each cluster/service
Acquired by Palo Alto Networks 2023 — product direction may change
Free tier limited to 1 system — enterprise pricing for production use
Decision logs are async stream — not available in real-time via REST API polling
Policy bundles distributed to OPA agents — not evaluated in DAS itself

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token auth with RBAC roles. SOC2/ISO27001 certified. OPA is CNCF graduated (audited). SSO/SAML for enterprise users. Policy decisions are access control — highly sensitive. Decision logs contain authorization decisions (PII potential). Acquired by Palo Alto Networks — enterprise security DNA. US/EU data residency.
